description: Add a first-class hal verify --json command that runs configured shell verification checks, emits a stable verify-v1 machine-readable contract, gates required failures, reports optional warnings, captures artifacts, and records verification metadata for factory runs.

userStories.title: Define verify-v1 result types
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want centralized verification result types so that command output, contract examples, and factory records share one stable data model.
acceptanceCriteria: An internal verification package defines result types for schemaVersion, generatedAt, status, summary, checks, warnings, and artifacts.

Each check result type includes id, name, adapter, status, required, command, workDir, timeoutSeconds, startedAt, finishedAt, durationMs, exitCode, stdoutArtifact, stderrArtifact, and message JSON fields.
Top-level status values are represented as pass, fail, and warn.
Check status values are represented as pass, fail, timeout, missing, and skipped.
Result type JSON tags emit camelCase field names matching the verify-v1 contract.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document verify-v1 contract
userStories.description: As a factory integrator, I want a documented verification JSON contract so that automation can consume verification output without parsing human text.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/contracts/verify-v1.md documents the top-level JSON fields schemaVersion, generatedAt, status, summary, checks, warnings, and artifacts.
The contract documents every per-check field emitted by shell-command checks.
The contract documents valid top-level statuses as pass, fail, and warn.
The contract documents valid check statuses as pass, fail, timeout, missing, and skipped.
The contract documents that required check failures and timeouts produce a failing gate.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add verify-v1 example payloads
userStories.description: As a developer, I want checked-in example JSON payloads so that contract consumers can build against realistic outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one passing verify-v1 example payload is checked in.
At least one failing verify-v1 example payload is checked in.
At least one warning-only optional-check verify-v1 example payload is checked in.
Example payloads use the documented top-level field names exactly.
Example payloads use the documented per-check field names exactly.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Lock verify JSON field contract in tests
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want tests that lock the verify-v1 field names so that future refactors do not silently break machine consumers.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests assert the exact top-level JSON keys emitted by verification result serialization.
Tests assert the exact per-check JSON keys emitted for shell-command checks.
Tests verify marshal and unmarshal round-trip behavior for verification result types.
Tests fail when a documented required field is omitted from emitted JSON.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add verification configuration model
userStories.description: As a Hal user, I want to configure verification checks so that each project can define its own tests, lint, and typecheck commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Verification config supports multiple shell checks.
Each shell check supports id, name, command, workDir, timeoutSeconds, and required.
The required field defaults to true when omitted.
The workDir field defaults to the project root when omitted.
Missing id, name, command, or invalid timeoutSeconds values produce actionable validation errors.
Optional checks are represented with required set to false.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Execute passing shell checks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want configured shell checks to run successfully so that local verification can confirm the repo is ready for handoff.
acceptanceCriteria: A configured shell command that exits 0 produces a check result with status pass.
When all required checks pass and no optional checks warn, the top-level result has status pass.
The result includes command, workDir, startedAt, finishedAt, and durationMs metadata.
Shell check execution uses the configured working directory.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fail required shell checks
userStories.description: As a factory run, I want required check failures to fail verification so that broken changes are blocked before PR or CI handoff.
acceptanceCriteria: A required shell command that exits non-zero produces a check result with status fail.
The top-level result has status fail when any required check fails.
The check result includes the failing command exit code.
The summary counts include the failed required check.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce shell check timeouts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want verification checks to time out so that hung commands do not block factory runs indefinitely.
acceptanceCriteria: A shell check exceeding timeoutSeconds is terminated.
A timed-out required check produces status timeout.
The top-level result has status fail when a required check times out.
A timeout result includes a message naming the configured timeout.
The summary counts include timed-out checks.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Warn for optional check problems
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want optional checks to report problems without failing the gate so that advisory checks can be introduced gradually.
acceptanceCriteria: An optional check that exits non-zero produces a warning result without setting the top-level status to fail.
An optional check with a missing command or missing working directory produces status missing.
The top-level result has status warn when only optional checks fail, time out, or are missing.
Warning details are included in the warnings array.
The summary counts include warning-producing optional checks.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Capture verification artifacts
userStories.description: As a factory operator, I want verification output artifacts referenced in the result so that failures can be inspected after the command exits.
acceptanceCriteria: Shell-check stdout can be captured as an artifact reference.
Shell-check stderr can be captured as an artifact reference.
Artifact references are included in per-check stdoutArtifact and stderrArtifact fields when output is captured.
Artifact references are included in the top-level artifacts array.
For a check with id test, captured output artifacts are written to .hal/reports/verify/test-stdout.txt and .hal/reports/verify/test-stderr.txt so tests can assert exact paths.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add hal verify --json command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want a dedicated verify command so that I can run the same verification gate used by factory automation.
acceptanceCriteria: hal verify --json loads configured verification checks and runs them.
hal verify --json emits only valid verify-v1 JSON on stdout.
Human-facing warnings or diagnostics are written somewhere other than JSON stdout.
The command exits 0 when the top-level result status is pass.
The command exits 0 when the top-level result status is warn.
The command exits non-zero when the top-level result status is fail.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Record verification in factory runs
userStories.description: As a factory run, I want verification summaries and artifact references recorded as run events so that run history shows what was verified before handoff.
acceptanceCriteria: Factory run records can include a verification summary with total, passed, failed, timedOut, skipped, and warning counts.
Factory run records can reference verification artifacts emitted by hal verify --json.
Verification recording does not add queue, trigger, sandbox, policy, secrets, or handoff implementation.
Tests verify that a factory run record can include verification summary and artifact references.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add verify command documentation
userStories.description: As a user, I want documentation for configuring and running verification so that projects can adopt the new gate without reading source code.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI help documents hal verify --json.
Documentation includes a minimal shell-check configuration example.
Documentation explains required versus optional check behavior.
Documentation explains pass, warn, and fail gate outcomes.
Generated CLI docs include the hal verify command page and make docs-check passes.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
